seoTitle: How to Change the Width and Margin of a Strip in Wix
metaDescription: Learn how to adjust strip width, stretch behavior, and margin padding in Wix to control section spacing across devices.
intro: Getting a section's spacing right in Wix often comes down to understanding strip width and margin settings, which control whether content stretches full-width or stays within fixed bounds. This guide covers accessing the strip layout toolbar, toggling stretch behavior, and fine-tuning margin padding for a cleaner layout.

steps.heading: Select your strip container
steps.body: Click on the target structural strip section in your Wix editor.

steps.heading: Open the layout toolbar
steps.body: Click the floating layout button to access width and margin preferences.

steps.heading: Choose your stretch mode
steps.body: Toggle between full-screen width stretching and a fixed, page-bound frame.

steps.heading: Adjust outer margins
steps.body: Manually set left and right percentage padding to control the outer spacing.

steps.heading: Set inner spacing
steps.body: Add inner padding to push content away from the edges of the column.

steps.heading: Check mobile spacing and preview
steps.body: Toggle mobile view to confirm the margin adjustments look right on smaller screens, then preview across devices.

faq.question: What's the difference between full-width stretch and fixed width?
faq.answer: Full-width stretch lets a strip's background extend edge to edge across the screen, while fixed width keeps it constrained to your page's set boundaries.

faq.question: Why is my content touching the edge of the screen?
faq.answer: This usually means inner margin padding is set too low, so increasing it will push your elements away from the column edges.

faq.question: Do margin settings apply differently on mobile?
faq.answer: Yes, it's worth checking the mobile view separately since spacing that looks fine on desktop can feel cramped or excessive on smaller screens.

faq.question: Can I set exact pixel values for margins?
faq.answer: Yes, the layout panel supports custom pixel or percentage-based width and margin inputs depending on your settings.
